methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Cascade Space is a Seed-stage space & aerospace company founded by Jacob Portukalian and Arlen Abraham that has raised $5.9M to date. The target operates within a sector containing 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 identifiable private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin, though direct peer valuation data at Cascade's stage and scale is sparse.

Of three valuation methods examined, only Stage-Progression proved applicable. Using total capital raised ($5.9M) as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, this method implies a $15M valuation. Peer-Median analysis was not viable due to insufficient same-stage or similarly-sized comparables in the space sector—only 1 adjacent-stage data point was available against a minimum threshold of 3. Public-Comp Anchoring could not be applied because Cascade's revenue is not disclosed; while the Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 42.9x revenue across the 6 public tickers, the target's top-line figure is necessary to extrapolate.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $13M to $17M, centered on $15M. This estimate rests entirely on stage-progression mechanics and carries material uncertainty given the single-method foundation. Key risks include the appropriateness of the 2.5x multiplier for this specific company and sector conditions, and the absence of transaction or peer valuation precedent to validate the implied step-up magnitude.
